      "それは公立大学におけるシェイク・ムジブル・ラーマンに捧げられた最大の記念壁画であることが特徴です。",

      "それは学生が主張するために使用されます。"

IsA: It is a portrait wall painting.
HasA: It has a 5 feet high altar.
It has an additional 15-foot-wide space for movement on three sides of the structure.
It has a 4-foot-wide and 20-foot-high wall on the right side of the original portrait.
It has an English message signed by Sheikh Mujibur Rahman recorded on the wall with its translation in Bengali.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being the largest memorial mural dedicated to Sheikh Mujibur Rahman in public universities.
It is characterized by being the second largest memorial mural dedicated to Sheikh Mujibur Rahman in the country.
It is characterized by its dimensions of 50 feet in length and 38 feet in width.
It is characterized by a portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman that is 26 feet in height and 17 feet in width.
It is characterized by being made of carved stones and tiles.
UsedFor: It is used for commemorating Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.
It is used for increasing the practice of progressiveness among students.
It is used for transmitting the ideals of the founding father among students.
It is used for inculcating non-communal spirit among all students.
It is used for various functions of the university.
It is used for students to sit in their free time to chat and gossiping.
It is used for students to claim.
LocatedIn: It is located on the campus area of the Islamic University, Bangladesh.
It is located at the entrance through the main gate of the campus of the university in Kushtia district.
It is located in Kushtia District.
It is located in Jhenaidah District.
BuiltIn: It was built in the campus of Islamic University.
It was built in the Kushtia district.
BuiltBy: It was built by Kanak Kumar Pathak.
It was built by the university authority and an ex-student named Jalal Uddin Tuhin.
It was built by Alimuzzaman Tutul and Kanak Kumar Pathak.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the campus of Islamic University.
FoundedBy: It was founded by the university authority and an ex-student named Jalal Uddin Tuhin.
CreatedBy: It was created by Kanak Kumar Pathak.
CreatedIn: It was created in the Islamic University, Bangladesh.
CausedBy: It is caused by the Student–People's uprising in August 2024.
Affects: It affects the practice of progressiveness among students.
It affects the transmission of ideals among students.
It affects the inculcation of non-communal spirit among students.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a mural structure with its altar.
It consists of carved stones and tiles.
It consists of an English message and its Bengali translation.
PartOf: It is part of the Islamic University campus.
It is part of the Kushtia District.
It is part of the Student–People's uprising in August 2024.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Islamic University, Bangladesh.
It belongs to the Kushtia District.
KnownFor: It is known for being a tourist spot.
It is known for its large size.
It is known for commemorating Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.
It is associated with the Islamic University, Bangladesh.

It is associated with Abdul Hamid.
NamedAfter: It is named after Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.
HeldIn: It was held in the campus of the Islamic University, Bangladesh.

InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the love of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman.
It was influenced by the ideals of the founding father.
Influenced: It influenced the practice of progressiveness among students.
It influenced the transmission of ideals among students.
It influenced the inculcation of non-communal spirit among students.
